user.title: Modbus gateway
user.description: Connect to the Modbus TCP gateway (or a device speaking Modbus TCP directly).

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the Modbus TCP gateway.
data_description.port: TCP port of the gateway. Almost always 502.
data_description.slave_id: Unit/slave ID of the Modbus device behind the gateway (0–255).
data_description.prefix: Optional friendly name for this device. Leave empty to use manufacturer and model from the device file.

device.title: Device type
device.description: Pick the device definition that matches your hardware. Your own YAML files in the `modbus_connect` folder of the Home Assistant configuration directory are listed too.

data_description.filename: The YAML file describing the registers and entities of your device.

reconfigure.title: Modbus gateway
reconfigure.description: Update the connection to the Modbus TCP gateway.

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the Modbus TCP gateway.
data_description.port: TCP port of the gateway. Almost always 502.
data_description.slave_id: Unit/slave ID of the Modbus device behind the gateway (0–255).
data_description.prefix: Optional friendly name for this device. Leave empty to use manufacturer and model from the device file.

reconfigure_device.title: Device type
reconfigure_device.description: Pick the device definition that matches your hardware. Your own YAML files in the `modbus_connect` folder of the Home Assistant configuration directory are listed too.

data_description.filename: The YAML file describing the registers and entities of your device.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the gateway. Check host and port, and make sure the gateway is reachable.

abort.already_configured: A device with this gateway and Modbus device ID is already configured.
abort.no_device_files: No valid device definition files were found.

data_description.scan_interval: Default polling interval in seconds. The device file and individual entities can override this.
